title: DIY Napkin Holder
summary: In my garage, I have a big pile of scrap wood left over from other projects.
description: In my garage, I have a big pile of scrap wood left over from other projects. I didn’t want to throw it away so I decided to build a small DIY Napkin Holder with salt and pepper compartments. This is a simple DIY project for a beginner to try and doesn’t cost a lot of money to make it.

steps.title: Step-1 Cut Two Sides of a Napkin Ho
steps.body: Take ½” plywood and with a Table Saw cut two 2 1/2"x11 1/2" pieces with a 45 degree cut on the 2 1/2" side.

steps.title: Step-2 Drill a Hole Thru Both Sides
steps.body: From the inside of the 45° cut, measure and mark 3 1/2". Then from the top of the board measure and mark 1 1/4". This will be the center of the hole. Using a 1 1/4" hole saw bit drill a hole in the center of the mark. Then cut from top of the board to the hole opening with a jig saw. Do this for both boards.

steps.title: Step-3 Cut the Smaller Sides of the
steps.body: Similar to step one, take 1/2" plywood and cut two boards to 2 1/2" x 8" with 45° cut on the 2 1/2" side.

steps.title: Step-4 Attach all Sides Together
steps.body: Using the random orbital sander lightly sand the pieces to eliminate any rough edges. Then apply Wood Glue in the seams and attach the pieces together using Nail Gun with 1 ¼” brad nails. I only had one nail in the center of the board.

steps.title: Step-5 Cut 1/4" Plywood for Bottom
steps.body: Now take ¼” plywood and cut a 13 1/2" x 10" piece. Then place frame of the napkin holder on top of this 1/4" plywood making sure its aligned in the center. Apply Wood Glue between plywood and frame and clamp it together. Let the glue dry.

steps.title: Step-6 Make a Divider for Salt and
steps.body: To make a divider for the salt and pepper, cut one 7" x 2 1/2" piece and one 3" x 2 1/2" piece. Glue both pieces together as shown on the picture. This will be inserted in the napkin holder after it's stained.

steps.title: Step-7 Cut PVC Pipe for Napkin Hold
steps.body: Take a 3/4" PVC pipe and cut it to 10" in length. Then slide both PVC end caps on both ends.

steps.title: Step-8 Finish by Staining and Paint
steps.body: Stain and paint all the pieces including the PVC pipe. Once everything is stained, insert the salt and pepper divider inside the napkin holder frame and nail it using a Nail Gun. Then attach the decor metal pieces on each corner. You're done with this DIY napkin holder.
